title: Dogfood P1 state truthfulness E2E
goal: Implement the next P1 backlog slice end-to-end: native-subagent state hygiene, lane ownership/scope checks, and completed-run output fallback.

decisions.title: Bigger P1 E2E slice
decisions.rationale: These backlog items all protect operators from misleading workflow state, so they should be implemented and reviewed together against end-to-end state/TUI behavior.

decisions.title: One shared implementation lane
decisions.rationale: The features likely touch shared state, runner, TUI, and test files. Use one fixed worktree lane with sequential phases to avoid parallel write conflicts while still preserving durable workflow evidence.

decisions.title: Mandatory lead review after agents
decisions.rationale: Recent dogfood showed model review can miss state lifecycle and command-behavior gaps. The workflow prompts require process-level tests and the lead must perform final review before merge.

phases.name: implementation
phases.title: Implementation
phases.goal: Implement the P1 misleading-state prevention backlog slice.

checks.name: state truthfulness focused tests
checks.cmd: python3 -m pytest tests/test_workflow.py -q -k 'native or ownership or scope or output_fallback or completed_run or opaque or preview'

jobs.name: p1-state-truthfulness
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as the ccc @mimo25p implementation worker. Do not run ccc, do not spawn/delegate to another agent, and do not say you will use ccc @mimo25p. Edit files directly in this declared worktree lane. Load the pirfl skill from ~/.claude/skills/pirfl if no local copy is available.

Task: implement a bigger P1 workflow-state truthfulness slice for the workflow skill. Cover these backlog items together:

1. Native-subagent status/output integration: native Codex subagents should not be represented as misleading managed workers with no liveness/output. Either add first-class state fields/adapter conventions for native sidecars, or clearly classify them as unmanaged helpers in state/TUI/status. Do not encourage manual stale `running` agents.
2. Lane ownership/scope checks: compare changed files from worktree lanes against declared `write_scope` and surface warnings/checks before review/integration. This should catch workers changing files outside their owned scope.
3. Completed-run output fallback: completed/failed/cancelled runs should not look blank when final output artifacts are empty. TUI/status/preview should fall back to transcript snippets, latest output/thinking/todo/tool/error, cancellation reason, last status event, or artifact summaries. The runner should avoid treating empty final-output files as useful artifacts, or synthesize a clear summary.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add focused tests that reproduce misleading state before the fix and assert the corrected state/UI/preview behavior.
- Include at least one test that creates a completed worker with empty final output but useful transcript/event data and verifies a nonblank preview/fallback.
- Include at least one test that creates a lane with write_scope and out-of-scope changes and verifies a warning/check is recorded and visible.
- Include at least one test for native/unmanaged sidecar semantics so operators can distinguish native helpers from managed external workers.
- Keep existing opaque-agent invariant, process identity, TUI duration, worktree merge, and workflow apply tests passing.
- Update SKILL.md/references docs only where behavior changes or new conventions are introduced.

Likely files: scripts/workflow_health.py, scripts/workflow_ops.py, scripts/workflow_run_codex.py, scripts/workflow_tui*.py, scripts/workflow_state.py, tests/test_workflow.py, references/state-schema.md, references/operations.md, references/known-gaps-and-roadmap.md, SKILL.md. Preserve style and avoid broad refactors.

Run at minimum the new focused tests and WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 tests/test_workflow.py if practical. Commit your changes on the lane branch with message: feat(workflow): improve state truthfulness checks.

Return strict JSON with keys: status (complete|partial|blocked), files_changed, tests (array of command/result), behavior (array), blockers (array), last_commit, notes.

phases.name: structural-review
phases.title: Structural Review
phases.goal: Review state truthfulness implementation for concrete bugs and missing tests.

jobs.name: mm3-state-truthfulness-review
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as the ccc @mm3 review worker. Do not run ccc, do not spawn/delegate to another agent, and do not say you will use ccc @mm3. Work in the current cwd, which is the implementation worktree lane.

Review the P1 state truthfulness implementation. Focus on misleading-state bugs: stale native sidecars, out-of-scope lane changes, blank completed/cancelled previews, health/status/TUI consistency, and tests that assert actual state/rendered behavior rather than implementation details. If you find concrete issues, fix narrowly and commit with message: fix(workflow): address mm3 state truthfulness review. If no concrete issues, do not commit.

Run focused tests relevant to any fixes. Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|fixed|blocked), findings (array), tests (array), commits (array), notes.

phases.name: semantic-review
phases.title: Semantic Review
phases.goal: Deep semantic review of state lifecycle and operator-facing behavior.

jobs.name: glm51-state-truthfulness-review
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as the ccc @glm51 review worker. Do not run ccc, do not spawn/delegate to another agent, and do not say you will use ccc @glm51. Work in the current cwd.

Perform a deep semantic review of the P1 state truthfulness slice. Check state-schema compatibility, operator-facing status/TUI behavior, health/completion invariants, lane ownership false positives/false negatives, completed-output fallbacks, and native/unmanaged sidecar semantics. If you find concrete issues, fix narrowly and commit with message: fix(workflow): address glm51 state truthfulness review. If no concrete issues, do not commit.

Run focused tests and the fast suite if practical. Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|fixed|blocked), findings (array), tests (array), commits (array), notes.

phases.name: final-review
phases.title: Final Regression Review
phases.goal: Final regression review after implementation and model-diverse reviews.

jobs.name: mimo25p-state-truthfulness-final
jobs.prompt:
You are already running as the ccc @mimo25p final review worker. Do not run ccc, do not spawn/delegate to another agent, and do not say you will use ccc @mimo25p. Work in the current cwd.

Final review the P1 state truthfulness branch after prior review commits. Focus on regressions, docs, tests, and whether the feature is understandable for a lead operator dogfooding workflows. If you find concrete issues, fix narrowly and commit with message: fix(workflow): address final state truthfulness review. If no concrete issues, do not commit.

Run focused tests and WORKFLOW_TEST_MODE=fast python3 tests/test_workflow.py if practical. Return strict JSON with keys: status (pass|fixed|blocked), findings (array), tests (array), commits (array), notes.
